projectDescription: A Python DSL for writing handcrafted PTX kernels targeting NVIDIA Hopper and Blackwell GPUs, callable from JAX and PyTorch.
keyFeatures: One Python call = one PTX instruction — no hidden optimizer between your code and the PTX
Full Hopper ISA support: WGMMA, TMA 2D/3D multicast, mbarriers, cluster launch
Full Blackwell ISA support: tcgen05.mma, TMEM, SMEM descriptors, 2-SM cooperative MMA
PTX parser + emitter with byte-identical round-trip on 218+ real-world PTX files
PTX-to-Python transpiler to convert nvcc/Triton/Pallas output to editable pyptx DSL
Single kernel callable from JAX (jax.jit), PyTorch eager, and torch.compile
C++ dispatch shim for low-overhead kernel launches (~14 µs with ninja)
publicApiSurface: pyptx.kernel — @kernel decorator for tracing Python functions into PTX
pyptx.ptx — PTX instruction namespace (ptx.inst.*, ptx.wgmma.*, ptx.tcgen05.*, ptx.cp.async.*, ptx.mbarrier.*, etc.)
pyptx.reg — register allocation (reg.scalar, reg.array, reg.from_, reg.pred)
pyptx.smem — shared memory allocation (smem.wgmma_tile, smem.mbarrier, smem.alloc)
pyptx.Tile / pyptx.Layout — tensor boundary spec for @kernel in_specs/out_specs
pyptx.intrinsic — @intrinsic decorator for multi-instruction composable building blocks
pyptx.differentiable_kernel — wrap forward+backward kernels for torch.autograd
pyptx.types — PTX type objects: bf16, f32, f16, u32, s32, b32, b64, etc.
